page: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.2xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical Intel Xeon 6975P-C cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-threaded capabilities. The server achieves top-tier results in Geekbench text processing and strong performance in single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. Single-core Geekbench scores also place in the top 25%. Multi-core operations, such as stress-ng CPU tests, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores, fall within the average range. The processor includes advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512), which support accelerated vector and matrix operations.

From a resource density perspective, the flex designation represents a balanced hardware profile that optimizes compute power per resource unit without the added cost of bundled local storage. It is well-suited for compute-bound workloads that benefit from high single-core performance and specialized instruction sets, such as text processing, lightweight machine learning inference, web serving, and software compilation.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz, this Nitro-based virtual machine delivers strong single-core performance, particularly in text processing and single-threaded compression tasks. The hardware includes advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX for vector and matrix math acceleration. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying on a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. This configuration provides a cost-effective option for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, web serving, and lightweight LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB of DDR5 RAM, and strong single-core performance for text processing and compression workloads.

tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring high-speed DDR5 memory and advanced Intel instruction sets for efficient single-threaded processing.
